questions.question: What is a force?
questions.answer: A force is a push or pull that can change the state of motion or shape of an object.

questions.question: Give two examples of force in daily life.
questions.answer: Pushing a door and pulling a rope are examples of force.

questions.question: What can a force do to an object?
questions.answer: A force can move an object, stop it, change its direction, or change its shape.

questions.question: What is muscular force?
questions.answer: Muscular force is the force applied by the muscles of humans or animals.

questions.question: What is gravitational force?
questions.answer: Gravitational force is the force by which the Earth attracts objects towards itself.

questions.question: What is friction?
questions.answer: Friction is the force that opposes the motion of objects moving over a surface.

questions.question: Why does a rolling ball stop after some time?
questions.answer: A rolling ball stops due to friction between the ball and the ground.

questions.question: What is a contact force?
questions.answer: A contact force is a force that acts only when objects are in physical contact.

questions.question: Give one example of a contact force.
questions.answer: Friction is an example of a contact force.

questions.question: What is a non-contact force?
questions.answer: A non-contact force acts on objects without physical contact.

questions.question: Give one example of a non-contact force.
questions.answer: Gravitational force is a non-contact force.

questions.question: What happens when we push a stationary object?
questions.answer: The object may start moving when a force is applied.

questions.question: What happens when we apply force to a moving object?
questions.answer: The force may change the speed or direction of the object.

questions.question: What is magnetic force?
questions.answer: Magnetic force is the force by which magnets attract or repel objects.

questions.question: Give one example of magnetic force.
questions.answer: A magnet attracting iron nails is an example of magnetic force.

questions.question: What is electrostatic force?
questions.answer: Electrostatic force is the force between electrically charged objects.

questions.question: Give one example of electrostatic force.
questions.answer: A rubbed comb attracting small pieces of paper is an example of electrostatic force.

questions.question: What is meant by balanced forces?
questions.answer: Balanced forces are equal forces acting in opposite directions on an object.

questions.question: What is meant by unbalanced forces?
questions.answer: Unbalanced forces occur when the forces acting on an object are not equal.

questions.question: What happens when forces acting on an object are balanced?
questions.answer: The object remains at rest or continues moving at constant speed.

questions.question: What happens when forces acting on an object are unbalanced?
questions.answer: The motion of the object changes.

questions.question: Why are brakes used in vehicles?
questions.answer: Brakes use friction to slow down or stop vehicles.

questions.question: Why do objects fall to the ground?
questions.answer: Objects fall due to the gravitational force of the Earth.

questions.question: Can force change the shape of an object?
questions.answer: Yes, applying force can change the shape of objects like clay or rubber bands.

questions.question: Why is friction useful in daily life?
questions.answer: Friction helps us walk, hold objects, and control the motion of vehicles.
